content: We can reframe SOTA 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems as a built-in curiosity regulator for autonomous ICM-based agents, where hierarchical long-term memory prioritizes storing surprising, under-explored environmental patterns instead of just rewarding one-off novel observations. This creates a self-sustaining active learning loop that avoids the common 'novelty trap' where curious agents fixate on trivial repeating randomness instead of pursuing structured, generalizable knowledge.
